subject: Physics
title: The Patent Clerk Who Changed Physics
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Patent Clerk Who Changed Physics.

hook.text: In 1905, a 26-year-old patent clerk published four papers that rewrote the laws of physics.

buildup.text: Albert Einstein couldn't find a university job after graduating. He reviewed patents instead.

discovery.text: Working alone, he solved problems that had stumped the world's top physicists for decades.

twist.text: His professors had called him lazy. One wrote he would never amount to anything in physics.

climax.text: His 1905 papers covered light, atoms, special relativity, and E = mc². Physics was reborn.

punchline.text: A rejected academic. Four papers. Physics changed forever.

quiz.question: What was Einstein's job when he published his groundbreaking 1905 papers?
